explanation:
TRUE POSITIVE. The deposit() function calls _updatePoolRewards() at line 601 before updating investor.totalDeposit at line 609. Inside _updatePoolRewards(), a memory snapshot of the investor's info is taken (line 792) with the pre-deposit totalDeposit. When _checkAndAddInvestorToPool() evaluates pool criteria at line 866, it uses this stale value.

The critical impact occurs when: (1) the investor's old totalDeposit is below a pool's personalInvestRequired, (2) the new deposit would push it above, and (3) that pool is currently inactive. In this case, _checkAndAddInvestorToPool returns false, isAddedToPool is false, and the break at line 810 exits the loop — skipping the reward allocation (line 814: poolInfo.curReward += amount * poolInfo.share / BASIS_POINTS) for that pool and all higher pools.

This reward loss is permanent. No retroactive mechanism exists to allocate the missed reward contribution. While the investor can later be added to the pool on a subsequent deposit (after the 4-hour delay), the reward portion from the triggering deposit is irreversibly lost. Each skipped pool loses amount * share / BASIS_POINTS (up to 17.5% per pool) of the deposit's reward contribution.
